prose:
Method–provenance block
• Property: condensed Fukui functions (f⁺ electrophilic, f⁻ nucleophilic, f⁰ radical) and dual descriptor for aniline; headline number (reported above) is the largest electrophilic Fukui index, f⁺ = 2.2975867 on atom 3.
• Level of theory: DFT, tier `standard` = B3LYP/def2-TZVP with RI (density fitting) in PySCF 2.x.
• Geometry provenance: single-point charges on the fixed geometry supplied in `/Users/evane/Desktop/Research/Argonne/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/fukui-reactivity-validation/aniline/aniline.xyz`; no re-optimization was performed (DFT//input-geom).
• Solvent / environment: gas phase (no implicit or explicit solvation).
• Charge & multiplicity: neutral singlet reference (charge 0, multiplicity 1); cation (+1, doublet) and anion (–1, doublet) states for f±.
• Software: chemkit `fukui-reactivity` skill (PySCF backend). Command: `… cli.py fukui --method dft --tier standard aniline.xyz`. JSON output logged at `/var/folders/.../live.json`; live progress streamed to an `.out` file announced at launch.
• Convergence & caveats: all three SCFs converged; integrity status ok (`trustworthy: true`). Two engine warnings (echoed above) note charge-conservation checks; no fallbacks.

Key condensed Fukui indices (1-based atom labels):
  – Atom 3 (C, ortho to NH₂)  f⁺ = 2.2975867   (most electrophilic)
  – Atom 1 (N)               f⁻ = 0.20452359  (most nucleophilic)
  – Dual descriptor on atom 3: 2.2310153

Full per-atom data, Mulliken charges, and the diagnostic plot are in the result JSON and `..._fukui_reactivity/agent_call_01_plot.png`.
